name: runtime_script
description_lines: Run one small GDScript inspector or input-driver probe inside an active daemon-managed runtime_session.
Call runtime_session.start or runtime_session.status first and pass the returned session_id.
The script is saved, diagnosed, sent to the running scene through Fennara runtime helper, and the tool waits for that script to complete, fail, or time out.
Script contract:
- Runtime scripts are NOT editor tool scripts: never include `@tool`, and do not copy run_scene_edit_script templates into runtime_script.
- Scripts must extend RefCounted and define `func run(ctx: Variant) -> void`.
- Use explicit types for meaningful local variables.
- Use `ctx.log(...)` for observations.
- `await ctx.capture(label, max_resolution=1280)` immediately saves and publishes one viewport image, then returns a Dictionary receipt. It does not return the Image for composition.

- `await ctx.frame(max_resolution=1280) -> Image` waits for a renderable process frame and returns the current viewport as an unsaved Image. A positive max_resolution downsizes only when the longest side exceeds that value; pass 0 to preserve the viewport resolution.
- `ctx.output(image, description="") -> Dictionary` saves and publishes any non-empty Image without displaying it in the game viewport. Use it for completed sheets or other derived Images.
Image sequence and sheet workflow:

- Use `frame()` plus `sheet()` when many exact moments, poses, angles, or states are easier to understand together in one ordered image. Establish and pause each desired state yourself, append its Image in chronological or intentional order, compose pages, then publish only the completed pages.

- `ctx.sheet(images, options={}) -> Array[Image]` is a pure Image composer. It does not capture, advance time, change the scene, choose states, mutate source Images, display anything, save files, or publish output.
- `columns`: positive integer. Default is ceil(sqrt(image_count)).
- `cell_size`: Vector2i. Default is Vector2i(384, 216). Every cell has this size.
- `cells_per_sheet`: positive integer. Default is all supplied Images. Smaller values paginate the ordered input into multiple returned sheets.
- `labels`: optional Array indexed against the complete input sequence, including across pages. Compact uppercase labels are stamped after capture, so they do not contaminate the observed game.
- `gap`: non-negative integer pixel spacing. Default is 0.
- `fit`: `contain` preserves the complete image and may letterbox, `cover` preserves aspect ratio but center-crops, and `stretch` fills the cell by distorting it. Default is `contain`.
- `background`: Color used behind cells and letterboxing. Default is near-black Color(0.02, 0.02, 0.025, 1.0).
- `label_scale`: positive number controlling compact label size. Default is 1.0.
- Invalid sheet input records `ctx.error()` and returns an empty Array. Otherwise sheets are returned in deterministic row-major page order.
Minimal runtime sheet pattern:
```gdscript
var frames: Array[Image] = []
frames.append(await ctx.frame())
# Establish and pause the next exact state here.
frames.append(await ctx.frame())
var pages: Array[Image] = ctx.sheet(frames, {
"columns": 2,
for page_index: int in range(pages.size()):
ctx.output(pages[page_index], "Moments page %d" % (page_index + 1))

- Fennara saves every published output and reports its path. At most the first six images are attached to one tool result as model image context; additional outputs remain saved and are reported by path.
- For deeper camera, animation-state, and visual-comparison reasoning, read `res://addons/fennara/ai/visual-observation.md`.
- Scripts may complete without closing the scene; use follow-up runtime_script calls against the same running scene.
- Call `ctx.close_scene()` only for the final script when intentionally ending the runtime session, or use runtime_session.stop externally.
Project edit rule:
- Runtime testing can reveal real project bugs. It is valid to edit project code for real fixes or requested behavior changes.
- Do not change controller/input/scene/UI semantics merely to make a probe easier, then claim the original behavior worked.
- If project code changes during runtime testing, log what changed and verify the intended product behavior.
Runtime log contract:
- Keep logs token-efficient: compact milestones, sampled state, final summaries, and capture labels.
- In loops, accumulate counters/ranges/samples, then emit one concise summary.
- Fennara writes ctx logs, capture events, close requests, runtime errors, and completion markers into runtime_session.log with fixed `FENNARA_SCRIPT_*` prefixes.
- The tool result is an operational receipt with status, ids, script path, diagnostics, capture paths, runtime findings, and a capped excerpt of new runtime log lines.
- Use the receipt runtime log update first, and read the full runtime_session.log when older history or omitted text matters.
Ctx helper source rule:
- Before using any ctx helper that is not already proven in this runtime session, read the installed addon source under `res://addons/fennara/runtime/`.

- Start with runtime_script_context.gd for public ctx helper names, then read the relevant helper file: runtime_input_driver.gd for key/mouse/raw input, runtime_node_snapshot.gd for node/snapshot helpers, runtime_physics_query.gd for raycasts/scans, and runtime_query_utils.gd for target matching.
- Do not guess helper option names, coordinate spaces, return fields, or success meaning from the helper name.
Ctx helper map:

- log(), error(), wait(), capture(), frame(), sheet(), output(), restart_scene(), close_scene(), and get_scene_root() record observations, obtain or compose Images, publish selected Images, restart or end the runtime scene, and expose the current runtime root.
- node(), exists(), and snapshot() read live scene state. Use them to avoid stale references and to capture bounded properties or child lists.
- until(), until_exists(), until_missing(), and until_prop() wait for live state to become true instead of guessing timing.
- press_action(), release_action(), tap_action(), action(), and action_sequence() drive the project's InputMap actions exactly as the project defines them.

- press_key(), release_key(), tap_key(), hold_key(), press_mouse(), release_mouse(), tap_mouse(), hold_mouse(), input_event(), and release_all_inputs() send real Godot input events. Use raw input_event only after reading the event/property behavior.

- set_mouse_position(), move_mouse_to(), and move_mouse_relative() move the runtime mouse. world_to_viewport_2d() converts 2D world points to viewport mouse points; set_mouse_world_2d() aims/click-targets a 2D world point through the mouse.

- raycast_2d(), raycast_3d(), raycast_2d_scan(), and raycast_3d_scan() are sensing tools. Exact rays answer one line question; scans answer which sampled directions hit targets, blockers, or clear space. They do not act or prove success.
- click_at(), click_button(), find_button_by_text(), and button_path_by_text() interact with Control UI by screen position, node path, or button text.
- release_all_actions() and release_all_inputs() clean up held actions and raw inputs before exits, failures, timeouts, or direction changes.
Coordinate and input contracts:
- Mouse helpers that accept `position` use viewport mouse coordinates.
- If you have Node2D.global_position, a raycast hit, or any 2D world point, use world_to_viewport_2d() or set_mouse_world_2d() before mouse input.
- Do not pass Node2D/Node3D global_position directly to viewport mouse helpers unless the project reads that same coordinate space.
- Inspect the project code first: get_global_mouse_position(), get_viewport().get_mouse_position(), UI hit testing, and InputEventMouseMotion.relative are different input contracts.
- For scans, use the returned direction/object/hit position as sensing evidence, then choose project controls yourself. `matches_count` is ray matches, not object count.
Await rules:

- Await yielding helpers: wait, capture, frame, restart_scene, tap_action, action, action_sequence, tap_key, hold_key, tap_mouse, hold_mouse, move_mouse_to, move_mouse_relative, until, until_exists, until_missing, until_prop, click_at, and click_button.
- Without await, scripts can continue immediately, movement/click timing can be wrong, captures may not finish, and observations can be false.

- Synchronous helpers do not need await: log, error, sheet, output, press/release action, press/release key, press/release mouse, input_event, set_mouse_position, world_to_viewport_2d, set_mouse_world_2d, raycasts, node, exists, snapshot, release_all_actions, and release_all_inputs.
Runtime driving discipline:

- Before controlling a node or scene, inspect the scripts, input handlers, state properties, movement/aim/camera code, groups, paths, coordinate spaces, and real success signals that matter to that control.
- Use short observe -> act -> wait -> re-observe loops; keep every experiment bounded by time or iteration count.
- Re-fetch root, nodes, UI, physics targets, and state after every await, restart, reload, transition, queue_free(), or replacement.
- After `await ctx.restart_scene()`, discard every old node/root/reference and re-fetch from the new current scene before acting.
- Runtime sessions keep simulating between scripts; after planning delays, re-observe before acting.
- Release all actions/inputs on success, failure, timeout, and early return.
- Verify progress from real state changes, not attempted input counts.

code.description: Inline GDScript to save, diagnose, and run. Never include `@tool`; runtime_script is not run_scene_edit_script. Must extend RefCounted and define `func run(ctx: Variant) -> void`. Use explicit local types, await yielding ctx helpers, and use the top-level description_lines for helper choice patterns. Omit `ctx.close_scene()` for incremental probes; call it only when this script should close the runtime session. Pass either `code` or `script_path`, not both.

session_id.description: Required. The active managed runtime session id returned by `runtime_session.start` or `runtime_session.status`.

script_path.description: Existing `res://...gd` runtime script to diagnose and run. Use this to rerun or patch a previously returned script path. Omit `ctx.close_scene()` for incremental probes; call it only when this script should close the runtime session. Pass either `code` or `script_path`, not both.

timeout_ms.description: Optional wait budget for this script result. The scene can stay running after the script completes; use runtime_session.stop or a final script with `ctx.close_scene()` when done.
